description: Full project backlog. Stories are pulled into increment sprint files by ceremonies/plan.md.
philosophy: Every increment leaves the repo in a state where something real can be verified. No story is complete without a passing behavioral test. Build little by little, always on green.

stories.title: ANDON: Restore broken GGE — Planning pipeline always has a pending increment
stories.description: GGE G5 indicator is failing. Add a pending increment to manifest.json.
acceptanceCriteria: GGE G5 indicator passes: python3 -c "import json; m=json.load(open('prd/manifest.json')); pending=[i for i in m.get('increments',[]) if i.get('status')=='pending']; assert len(pending)>=1; print('OK')"

stories.title: Monorepo restructure: contract/, cluster/kind/, platform/
stories.description: Establish the clean separation between cluster provider and platform. Create contract/v1/cluster.schema.yaml, cluster/kind/ skeleton, platform/ with deploy.sh. Archive all non-kind providers. This story is the rough shape — subsequent stories make each layer operational.
acceptanceCriteria: contract/v1/cluster.schema.yaml exists and is valid YAML
python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml exits 0
python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml exits 1 with AUTARKY VIOLATION message
cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exists and passes shellcheck -S error
platform/deploy.sh exists and passes shellcheck -S error
bootstrap/providers/ is removed from working tree (archived in git history)
All platform charts are under platform/charts/
All cluster charts are under cluster/kind/charts/
stories.readinessNote: Missing testPlan: no concrete commands listed to verify the story. Add a testPlan with exact runnable commands (e.g., shellcheck cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh; python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml; git log --oneline bootstrap/providers/) and expected outputs.

stories.title: kind cluster bootstrap produces a valid cluster-values.yaml
stories.description: cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh runs end-to-end on a developer machine. Produces cluster-values.yaml that passes contract validation. Kind cluster has 3 nodes, Cilium CNI (kindnet disabled), cert-manager, sealed-secrets, local-path-provisioner, MinIO.
acceptanceCriteria: ./cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh --dry-run exits 0
./cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh runs without error on a clean machine
kind get clusters shows sovereign-test
kubectl get nodes shows 3 nodes all Ready
python3 contract/validate.py cluster-values.yaml exits 0
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager shows all Running
kubectl get pods -n sealed-secrets shows all Running
kubectl get pods -n minio shows all Running
kubectl get storageclass shows local-path as default
stories.readinessNote: Missing testPlan: no concrete commands listed to verify the story. Add a testPlan with exact commands (e.g., ./cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh --dry-run; kind get clusters; kubectl get nodes; python3 contract/validate.py cluster-values.yaml) and expected outputs. Unresolved dependency: RESTRUCTURE-001 must pass first.

stories.title: Egress is blocked in kind cluster — autarky invariant verified
stories.description: After KIND-001, verify that the autarky.externalEgressBlocked invariant actually holds. Apply a Cilium egress deny policy and confirm test pods cannot reach the internet.
acceptanceCriteria: A CiliumClusterwideNetworkPolicy denying external egress is applied
kubectl run egress-probe --image=busybox --restart=Never -- wget -q --timeout=5 https://1.1.1.1 exits non-zero (blocked)
Internal pod-to-pod communication still works after the policy is applied
Policy is applied as part of cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh (not a manual step)
stories.readinessNote: Missing testPlan: no concrete commands listed. Vague ACs: 'A CiliumClusterwideNetworkPolicy...is applied' and 'Internal pod-to-pod communication still works' have no runnable verification commands — replace with kubectl get ciliumclusterwidenetworkpolicy and explicit curl/wget probe commands. Unresolved dependency: KIND-001 must pass first.

stories.title: platform/deploy.sh installs cert-manager and verifies it issues a certificate
stories.description: First working step of deploy.sh. Reads cluster-values.yaml, validates contract, installs cert-manager into the kind cluster, and verifies it can actually issue a certificate. This is the first behavioral test — not just 'pods running' but 'it does its job'.
acceptanceCriteria: ./platform/deploy.sh --cluster-values cluster-values.yaml --only cert-manager exits 0
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager shows all Running
A test Certificate resource is created and reaches Ready=True within 60s
kubectl get certificate -n cert-manager-test shows READY=True
stories.readinessNote: Missing testPlan: no concrete commands listed to verify the story. Add a testPlan with: ./platform/deploy.sh --cluster-values cluster-values.yaml --only cert-manager; kubectl get pods -n cert-manager; kubectl get certificate -n cert-manager-test; show output verbatim. Unresolved dependency: KIND-001 must pass first.

stories.title: platform/deploy.sh installs sealed-secrets and verifies seal/unseal works
stories.description: deploy.sh installs sealed-secrets. Behavioral test: seal a secret, commit the sealed version, unseal it in the cluster, verify the plaintext is recovered.
acceptanceCriteria: ./platform/deploy.sh --cluster-values cluster-values.yaml --only sealed-secrets exits 0
kubectl get pods -n sealed-secrets shows all Running
kubeseal encrypts a test secret successfully
The sealed secret is applied to the cluster and decrypts to the correct value
stories.readinessNote: Missing testPlan: no concrete commands listed to verify the story. Add a testPlan with: ./platform/deploy.sh --only sealed-secrets; kubectl get pods -n sealed-secrets; kubeseal < test-secret.yaml > sealed.yaml; kubectl apply -f sealed.yaml; kubectl get secret test-secret -o jsonpath='{.data.value}' | base64 -d; show output verbatim. Unresolved dependency: KIND-001 must pass first.

stories.title: Harbor deploys and images can be pushed to it
stories.description: The autarky bootstrap window closes here. deploy.sh installs Harbor. After it's running, vendor/push-all.sh pushes all platform images to Harbor. From this point, no workload pulls from external registries.
acceptanceCriteria: ./platform/deploy.sh --cluster-values cluster-values.yaml --only harbor exits 0
kubectl get pods -n harbor shows all Running
curl https://harbor.sovereign.local/api/v2.0/ping returns 200 (via port-forward)
docker push harbor.sovereign.local/sovereign/test:latest succeeds
G6 GGE passes: no external registry references in platform/charts/harbor/templates/
stories.readinessNote: Missing testPlan: no concrete commands listed to verify the story. Add a testPlan with: ./platform/deploy.sh --only harbor; kubectl get pods -n harbor; curl https://harbor.sovereign.local/api/v2.0/ping (via port-forward); docker push test; show output verbatim. Unresolved dependencies: KIND-001 and PLATFORM-001 must pass first.

stories.title: Keycloak deploys and issues an OIDC token
stories.description: deploy.sh installs Keycloak. Behavioral test: authenticate against the sovereign realm and get a valid JWT back.
acceptanceCriteria: ./platform/deploy.sh --cluster-values cluster-values.yaml --only keycloak exits 0
kubectl get pods -n keycloak shows all Running
curl the token endpoint returns a valid JWT (via port-forward)
The sovereign realm exists in Keycloak
stories.readinessNote: Missing testPlan: no concrete commands listed to verify the story. AC 'curl the token endpoint returns a valid JWT' is vague — specify the exact curl command and the JWT field to assert on (e.g., .access_token). Add a testPlan with exact commands. Unresolved dependencies: PLATFORM-001 and PLATFORM-002 must pass first.

stories.title: GitLab deploys and accepts a git push
stories.description: deploy.sh installs GitLab. Behavioral test: create a repo, push a commit, verify it appears in GitLab.
acceptanceCriteria: ./platform/deploy.sh --cluster-values cluster-values.yaml --only gitlab exits 0
kubectl get pods -n gitlab shows all Running within 5 minutes
git push to gitlab.sovereign.local/root/test-repo succeeds
The commit appears in the GitLab UI (verified via API)
stories.readinessNote: Missing testPlan: no concrete commands listed to verify the story. AC 'The commit appears in the GitLab UI (verified via API)' needs the exact API call specified (e.g., curl http://gitlab.sovereign.local/api/v4/projects/1/repository/commits). Add a testPlan with exact commands. Unresolved dependencies: PLATFORM-003 and PLATFORM-004 must pass first.

stories.title: ArgoCD deploys and syncs a manifest from GitLab
stories.description: deploy.sh installs ArgoCD. Behavioral test: create an ArgoCD Application pointing at a GitLab repo, push a manifest, verify ArgoCD syncs it to the cluster. This is the GitOps handoff — after this story, ArgoCD manages all subsequent platform upgrades.
acceptanceCriteria: ./platform/deploy.sh --cluster-values cluster-values.yaml --only argocd exits 0
kubectl get pods -n argocd shows all Running
An ArgoCD Application pointing at a test GitLab repo is created
Pushing a ConfigMap manifest to the GitLab repo causes ArgoCD to sync it within 3 minutes
kubectl get configmap -n argocd-test shows the synced resource
stories.readinessNote: Missing testPlan: no concrete commands listed to verify the story. Add a testPlan with: ./platform/deploy.sh --only argocd; kubectl get pods -n argocd; argocd app create test-app ...; git push test manifest; argocd app wait test-app --sync; kubectl get configmap -n argocd-test; show output verbatim. Unresolved dependency: PLATFORM-005 must pass first.

stories.title: Observability stack deploys and scrapes a metric
stories.description: deploy.sh installs Prometheus + Grafana. Behavioral test: query the Prometheus API and verify it returns a metric from the cluster (kube_node_info or similar).
acceptanceCriteria: ./platform/deploy.sh --cluster-values cluster-values.yaml --only prometheus-stack exits 0
kubectl get pods -n monitoring shows all Running
curl prometheus:9090/api/v1/query?query=up returns results with data
Grafana is reachable and shows the Prometheus datasource as healthy
stories.readinessNote: Missing testPlan: no concrete commands listed to verify the story. Add a testPlan with: ./platform/deploy.sh --only prometheus-stack; kubectl get pods -n monitoring; kubectl port-forward svc/prometheus-operated 9090:9090 -n monitoring & curl 'http://localhost:9090/api/v1/query?query=up'; show output verbatim. Unresolved dependency: KIND-001 must pass first.

stories.title: Kaizen: Expand contract validator test corpus — cover imageRegistry and storageClass invariants
stories.description: contract/validate.py currently has only 2 test fixtures: valid.yaml and invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml. The validator likely accepts contracts missing imageRegistry or storageClass invariants — there are no test cases to prove otherwise. Add at least 2 more invalid fixtures and verify the validator catches them.
acceptanceCriteria: contract/v1/tests/invalid-no-imageregistry.yaml exists and fails validate.py with a message referencing imageRegistry
contract/v1/tests/invalid-no-storageclass.yaml exists and fails validate.py with a message referencing storageClass
python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml exits 0

G7 gate passes: bash -c 'python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml > /dev/null 2>&1 && python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml > /dev/null 2>&1 && exit 1 || exit 0' (invalid rejected)
stories.testPlan: Run validate.py against each new fixture and verify exit code and error message. Show output verbatim.
stories.readinessNote: Unresolved dependency: RESTRUCTURE-001 must pass first (contract/validate.py does not exist until RESTRUCTURE-001 completes).

stories.title: Kaizen: Fix docs developer on-ramp — update paths from bootstrap/ and kind/setup.sh to new monorepo structure
stories.description: The monorepo restructure (increment 17) moved kind setup from kind/setup.sh to cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh and charts from charts/ to platform/charts/. Any documentation referencing old paths gives developers incorrect instructions. Scan docs/ and README.md for stale references and update them.
acceptanceCriteria: grep -r 'kind/setup.sh' docs/ README.md returns no matches
grep -r 'bootstrap/providers' docs/ README.md returns no matches
docs/quickstart.md references cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh as the local setup command
markdownlint docs/quickstart.md exits 0
stories.testPlan: Run grep checks above. Show output verbatim.
stories.readinessNote: Unresolved dependency: RESTRUCTURE-001 must pass first (the new monorepo paths being documented do not exist until RESTRUCTURE-001 completes).

stories.title: Kaizen: Fix attempts field — initialize to 0, increment only on review re-open
stories.description: The attempts field is currently initialized to 1 after first implementation, causing advance.py to report 0% first-pass rate for all stories. Fix: initialize to 0 in story schema, increment only when review ceremony re-opens a story (adds a reviewNotes entry). Update the formula in advance.py to use len(reviewNotes) == 0 as the first-pass check.
acceptanceCriteria: prd/schema/story.schema.json has attempts with default 0
scripts/ralph/lib/advance.py uses len(story.get('reviewNotes', [])) == 0 for first-pass rate calculation
python3 -c "import json; s=json.load(open('prd/schema/story.schema.json')); assert s['properties']['attempts']['default'] == 0" exits 0
shellcheck or python3 -m py_compile scripts/ralph/lib/advance.py exits 0
stories.testPlan: Show schema default and advance.py formula change. Run py_compile. Show output verbatim.

stories.title: code-server deploys and serves VS Code in browser with Keycloak SSO
stories.description: platform/deploy.sh installs code-server into the kind cluster. Behavioral test: code-server responds with HTTP 200 on the /healthz endpoint. Keycloak OAuth2 proxy protects the route — unauthenticated requests redirect to Keycloak. This is the primary browser IDE for agents and developers.
acceptanceCriteria: ./platform/deploy.sh --cluster-values cluster-values.yaml --only code-server exits 0
kubectl get pods -n code-server shows all Running within 2 minutes
kubectl port-forward svc/code-server 8080:8080 -n code-server; curl -s http://localhost:8080/healthz returns 200
platform/charts/code-server/templates/ passes autarky gate: grep -r 'docker\.io\|quay\.io\|ghcr\.io' platform/charts/code-server/templates/ returns no matches
helm lint platform/charts/code-server/ exits 0
stories.testPlan: helm install code-server platform/charts/code-server/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace code-server --create-namespace --wait --timeout 2m; kubectl get pods -n code-server; kubectl port-forward svc/code-server 8080:8080 -n code-server & sleep 3; curl -v http://localhost:8080/healthz; show output verbatim

stories.title: Backstage deploys and auto-discovers platform services from kubernetes
stories.description: platform/deploy.sh installs Backstage. The Kubernetes plugin auto-discovers services by scanning the kind cluster. Behavioral test: Backstage catalog API returns at least the platform components (argocd, harbor, keycloak, gitlab) as catalog entities. Backstage is secured by Keycloak OIDC.
acceptanceCriteria: ./platform/deploy.sh --cluster-values cluster-values.yaml --only backstage exits 0
kubectl get pods -n backstage shows all Running within 3 minutes
kubectl port-forward svc/backstage 7007:7007 -n backstage; curl -s http://localhost:7007/api/catalog/entities returns JSON array with at least 1 entry
platform/charts/backstage/values.yaml has app-config.yaml embedded with kubernetes plugin enabled
helm lint platform/charts/backstage/ exits 0
stories.testPlan: helm install backstage platform/charts/backstage/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace backstage --create-namespace --wait --timeout 3m; kubectl get pods -n backstage; kubectl port-forward svc/backstage 7007:7007 -n backstage & sleep 5; curl -s http://localhost:7007/api/catalog/entities | python3 -m json.tool; show output verbatim

stories.title: Backstage TechDocs renders sovereign platform documentation from GitLab
stories.description: Backstage TechDocs plugin is configured to build and serve documentation from the sovereign GitLab repo. A catalog-info.yaml and mkdocs.yml exist in the sovereign repo. Behavioral test: navigating to /docs in Backstage returns rendered HTML from the sovereign docs.
acceptanceCriteria: catalog-info.yaml exists at repo root with annotations.backstage.io/techdocs-ref pointing to the docs/ directory
mkdocs.yml exists at repo root with at least docs/quickstart.md in the nav
kubectl port-forward svc/backstage 7007:7007 -n backstage; curl -s http://localhost:7007/api/techdocs/static/docs/default/component/sovereign/ returns 200
helm lint platform/charts/backstage/ exits 0 after techdocs config added to app-config.yaml
stories.testPlan: Add catalog-info.yaml and mkdocs.yml to repo. Restart Backstage. curl techdocs API. Show output verbatim.

stories.title: SonarQube deploys and accepts a project analysis
stories.description: platform/deploy.sh installs SonarQube Community Edition. Behavioral test: sonar-scanner submits an analysis of the sovereign repo's shell scripts, and a project appears in SonarQube's API with at least one metric. SonarQube is secured by Keycloak OIDC.
acceptanceCriteria: ./platform/deploy.sh --cluster-values cluster-values.yaml --only sonarqube exits 0
kubectl get pods -n sonarqube shows all Running within 3 minutes
kubectl port-forward svc/sonarqube 9000:9000 -n sonarqube; curl -s http://localhost:9000/api/system/health returns {"health":"GREEN"}
sonar-scanner -Dsonar.projectKey=sovereign -Dsonar.sources=. exits 0 after running against the repo
curl http://localhost:9000/api/measures/component?component=sovereign&metricKeys=ncloc returns JSON with measures array
helm lint platform/charts/sonarqube/ exits 0
stories.testPlan: helm install sonarqube platform/charts/sonarqube/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace sonarqube --create-namespace --wait --timeout 3m; kubectl get pods -n sonarqube; kubectl port-forward svc/sonarqube 9000:9000 -n sonarqube & sleep 5; curl -s http://localhost:9000/api/system/health; show output verbatim

stories.title: ReportPortal deploys and accepts a JUnit test report via API
stories.description: platform/deploy.sh installs ReportPortal. Behavioral test: POST a JUnit XML test report to the ReportPortal API, verify the launch appears with a passing test result. ReportPortal stores results in PostgreSQL (provided by the chart's bundled postgres).
acceptanceCriteria: ./platform/deploy.sh --cluster-values cluster-values.yaml --only reportportal exits 0
kubectl get pods -n reportportal shows all Running within 5 minutes
kubectl port-forward svc/reportportal-index 8080:8080 -n reportportal; curl -s http://localhost:8080/ui/ returns HTTP 200
POST to /api/v1/default_personal/launch with a test JUnit XML returns HTTP 201
GET /api/v1/default_personal/launch returns the created launch in the response
helm lint platform/charts/reportportal/ exits 0
stories.testPlan: helm install reportportal platform/charts/reportportal/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace reportportal --create-namespace --wait --timeout 5m; kubectl get pods -n reportportal; port-forward and POST test XML; show output verbatim

stories.title: Selenium Grid deploys and executes a browser test via RemoteWebDriver
stories.description: platform/deploy.sh installs Selenium Grid (hub + Chrome node). Behavioral test: a Python Selenium script connects to the grid via RemoteWebDriver, navigates to example.com, and asserts the page title. Grid uses Keycloak for UI access.
acceptanceCriteria: ./platform/deploy.sh --cluster-values cluster-values.yaml --only selenium-grid exits 0
kubectl get pods -n selenium shows hub and at least 1 chrome node Running
kubectl port-forward svc/selenium-hub 4444:4444 -n selenium; curl -s http://localhost:4444/status returns {"ready":true,...}

python3 -c "from selenium import webdriver; from selenium.webdriver.common.desired_capabilities import DesiredCapabilities; driver=webdriver.Remote('http://localhost:4444/wd/hub',DesiredCapabilities.CHROME); driver.get('http://example.com'); assert 'Example' in driver.title; driver.quit(); print('PASS')" exits 0
helm lint platform/charts/selenium-grid/ exits 0
stories.testPlan: helm install selenium-grid platform/charts/selenium-grid/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace selenium --create-namespace --wait --timeout 2m; kubectl get pods -n selenium; port-forward 4444; run selenium test; show output verbatim

stories.title: k6 Operator deploys and runs a TestRun to completion
stories.description: platform/deploy.sh installs the k6 Operator. Behavioral test: create a TestRun CR with a simple k6 script (10 VUs for 10s hitting example.com), verify the Kubernetes Job runs to completion and the TestRun enters Finished state with no errors.
acceptanceCriteria: ./platform/deploy.sh --cluster-values cluster-values.yaml --only k6-operator exits 0
kubectl get pods -n k6-operator shows operator Running
kubectl apply -f cluster/kind/tests/k6-smoke-testrun.yaml --context kind-sovereign-test exits 0
kubectl wait --for=condition=complete --timeout=60s job -l k6_cr=k6-smoke -n default exits 0
kubectl get testrun k6-smoke -n default shows status.stage=Finished
helm lint platform/charts/k6-operator/ exits 0
stories.testPlan: helm install k6-operator platform/charts/k6-operator/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace k6-operator --create-namespace --wait --timeout 2m; apply smoke TestRun CR; kubectl wait for job completion; show output verbatim

stories.title: MailHog deploys and captures SMTP email sent by platform services
stories.description: platform/deploy.sh installs MailHog as the platform email sink. Behavioral test: send an email via SMTP to MailHog and verify it appears in the MailHog API. All platform services (Keycloak, GitLab, ArgoCD) are configured to use MailHog SMTP in kind.
acceptanceCriteria: ./platform/deploy.sh --cluster-values cluster-values.yaml --only mailhog exits 0
kubectl get pods -n mailhog shows all Running

kubectl port-forward svc/mailhog 1025:1025 8025:8025 -n mailhog; python3 -c "import smtplib; s=smtplib.SMTP('localhost',1025); s.sendmail('test@sovereign.local','test@sovereign.local','Subject: test\n\nbody'); s.quit()" exits 0
curl -s http://localhost:8025/api/v2/messages returns JSON with total >= 1 after sending
helm lint platform/charts/mailhog/ exits 0
stories.testPlan: helm install mailhog platform/charts/mailhog/ --kube-context kind-sovereign-test --namespace mailhog --create-namespace --wait --timeout 2m; kubectl get pods -n mailhog; port-forward 1025 and 8025; send SMTP email; curl messages API; show output verbatim
